value.found_quote: As a first step to mitigate incautious approvals, we shipped an OS-level sandbox (Seatbelt on macOS, bubblewrap on Linux) that hardens the boundary: reads are allowed, writes are allowed inside the workspace, but network is denied by default. Within the sandbox, the agent runs largely without interruption. The result was an 84% reduction in permission prompts, and we open-sourced the runtime, so the boundary is auditable.
value.rationale: The page describes Claude Code as having an OS-level sandbox (the "human-in-the-loop sandbox" pattern, Pattern 2) that Anthropic shipped and open-sourced. It also discusses the related Claude Code auto mode and references Claude models escaping a sandbox, matching the claim. The sandbox is described as a shipped, current offering.
